title: Wowvideo of Reis monologue in 13 languages
display_title: Wowvideo of Reis monologue in 13 languages
wikitext:
[]: Wow...video of "Rei's monologue" in 13 languages..             Quote Post by V » Sat Nov 17, 2007 8:42 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yWTZbg32leM Someone spliced together different dub tracks of Rei's Monologue in Episode 14, from THIRTEEN different dubs; it's just two minutes long (each new sentence is in a new language). english, german, french, italian, catalan, spanish (european), portuguese (european), spanish (mexican), thai, korean, cantonese, chinese and Japanese

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Nov 17, 2007 9:00 pm I saw that a long time ago, and the Catalan VA got a lot of rave reviews from watchers.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 17, 2007 10:00 pm I saw that awhile ago too. I thought it was pretty cool.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Mon Nov 19, 2007 5:27 am lol at Catalan.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Mon Nov 19, 2007 5:49 am That was pretty cool. Some sounded like Rei being an older woman, definitely not her age.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Mon Nov 19, 2007 5:54 pm The French one sounds more like a Ritsuko to me. Very cool though.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:34 pm It's really more of a poem than a monologue... *giggles and runs away*

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Nov 19, 2007 9:31 pm This is the kind of thing that makes me wanna say get a life , but really I can't...

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Nov 19, 2007 9:32 pm the Catalan was terrible.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Nov 19, 2007 9:34 pm slothen wrote: the Catalan was terrible.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 19, 2007 9:34 pm BrikHaus wrote: Matt Greenfield said so.

[]:             Quote Post by bkquk » Wed Jul 17, 2013 1:03 pm Rei sounds like a old woman in Catalan , But it is interesting to hear Rei voice in different languages.

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Wed Jul 17, 2013 1:12 pm So that's what Rei sounds like in the German dub. I've been trying to secure a cheap copy of it, but I can't find one sub-€100. Really, very interesting. I kinda wish they did the monologue in full in each language, instead of just a few seconds.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 17, 2013 1:20 pm All around the world, Rei is a heavy smoker. Raise your hand if you'd make love to Italian Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Katana » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:53 pm Chuckman wrote: Raise your hand if you'd make love to Italian Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by bkquk » Wed Jul 17, 2013 4:16 pm Chuckman wrote: All around the world, Rei is a heavy smoker. Raise your hand if you'd make love to Italian Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 17, 2013 4:22 pm She probably bums them off Ritsuko.

[]:             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Wed Jul 17, 2013 5:25 pm Wow, I´m surprised. The portuguese version isn´t bad. It´s actualy okay! The fun thing is, I didn´t even know a portuguese version existed. I always tought there was only a portuguese(brazilian) version.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Wed Jul 17, 2013 6:34 pm tbh I think that Rei's voice only works in Japanese, Korean, Cantonese, and for some reason Spanish.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Wed Jul 17, 2013 8:41 pm Katana wrote: "Fiori... Molte cose uguali. Molte cose inutili. Cielo... rosso. Cielo rosso. Il colore rosso. Odio il colore rosso." Still better than the english dub, sounds even better than the Japanese dub IMO. You should thank Valentina Mari.
summary: []: Wow...video of "Rei's monologue" in 13 languages..             Quote Post by V » Sat Nov 17, 2007 8:42 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yWTZbg32leM Someone spliced together different dub tracks of Rei's Monologue in Episode 14, from THIRTEEN different dubs; it's just two minutes long (each new sentence is in a new language). english, german, french, italian, catalan, spanish (european), portuguese &#40
